disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound new whole-body weakness and loss of the ability to stand, transfer and mobilise, appearing in the 48 hours since discharge from a two-week hospital admission.
trigger_question_examples: What has changed since she came home?
How is she moving around at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Since she got home two days ago she can't stand or get to the toilet without help — she was independent before the hospital and now her legs just give way.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Dizziness and lightheadedness on standing that settles on sitting back down — patient-reportable postural symptom.
trigger_question_examples: Does she feel dizzy or lightheaded when she stands up?
What happens when she first gets to her feet?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — the moment she stands up her head swims and she feels like she'll pass out, then it eases when she sits down again.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A recent hospital admission for pneumonia with 14 days of bed rest, discharged two days ago.
trigger_question_examples: Has she been in hospital recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She was in for two weeks with pneumonia and mostly in bed the whole time; she only came home the day before yesterday.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A blood pressure tablet was recently increased in dose, and a course of antibiotics was started in hospital — current medications the patient/carer can state.
trigger_question_examples: What medications is she taking, and has anything changed recently?
Were any of her tablets started or increased in hospital?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Her blood pressure tablet dose was put up not long ago, and she's on a course of antibiotics they started in hospital.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Poor appetite and reduced oral intake with likely weight loss over the admission, plus mild new slowness answering questions.
trigger_question_examples: How has her eating and drinking been?
Is she her usual self when she talks to you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She's barely eaten since hospital and seems to have lost weight, and she's a bit slower than usual answering.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Near-misses with falling while trying to reach the bathroom, with an unsafe home environment (stairs, shower over bath) and inadequate immediate support.
trigger_question_examples: Has she had any falls or near-falls?
Is the home safe for her at the moment?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She's nearly gone over twice getting to the toilet, the bedroom's upstairs and the shower's over the bath — it's not safe for her like this.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negative screen the carer can report: no fever or productive cough, breathing comfortable at rest, SpO2 not measured by device but she is not blue or gasping.
trigger_question_examples: Any fever, or a cough bringing up phlegm?
Is she short of breath sitting still?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever that we can tell and no real cough now — she's puffed when she moves but she's comfortable sitting still.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negative screen: no one-sided calf swelling, redness, heat or calf pain; the weakness is in both legs equally.
trigger_question_examples: Is one calf swollen, red, hot or sore?
Is the weakness worse on one side?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, both legs are the same — no swelling or redness in one leg, and no sore calf.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negative screen: no facial droop, no slurred speech, no one-sided arm or leg weakness, no chest pain.
trigger_question_examples: Any drooping of her face, trouble speaking, or weakness only on one side?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No droop or slurred speech, and it's not just one side — it's both legs. No chest pain.
